findings.description: The HTML uses 'MEMORANDUM' as the h1 title, which is appropriate, but the subsequent section headings (I, II, III, IV) are rendered as h2 elements. This is acceptable, though the document structure in the PDF uses Roman numeral headings that are bold section titles rather than hierarchical sub-sections of the memorandum title.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'I. History/Background', 'II. March 14, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary', etc. are section headers. HTML renders them as h2, which is a reasonable structural choice.

findings.description: The first image has a descriptive alt text that is somewhat fabricated in specificity ('North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality document background'), while the remaining images have empty or non-descriptive alt text.
findings.evidence: HTML: alt='North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality document background' for first image; alt='' for subsequent images. The first image is the NC DEQ letterhead/logo area, so the alt text is reasonable but slightly embellished. The empty alt texts for subsequent images are for what appear to be page background images.

findings.description: The HTML contains embedded background images (5 total) that appear to represent page-level rendering artifacts from the PDF. These are not content images from the PDF but rather full-page background captures.
findings.evidence: HTML contains 5

findings.description: The Attachments section in the PDF lists attachments with Roman numerals I through VI, but the HTML also uses Roman numerals I through VI. However, the PDF page 2 lists them differently in the outline (I-IV for the report sections), and the attachment list on page 5 uses I-VI. The HTML correctly represents both.
findings.evidence: PDF outline on page 1: I-IV. Attachments list on page 5: I-VI. HTML captures both correctly.
